title: FAQ 8 - How to Walk Away from Payroll Debt
full_text: What strategies exist to eliminate payroll debt without bankruptcy? Sell me your corporation. That's it. That's it. Corporations are great that way. You sell them in a share sale. All your debt is gone. Gone. Corporate share sales. That's the sub question here. Yes. Debt assumption by a third party. Not possible without a share sale. Legal settlements or appeals. Why the hell would you do that? Why would you spend money? thanks for the hat why would you spend money anybody out there to have a lawyer try to negotiate with the CRA to try to get a lower rate they're not going to negotiate because they hold the cards but if you actually have the money you hold the cards why would you waste your time and money with that just sell me the company start over save your money put millions in your pocket by not giving it to them you know we have a client I'm not going to tell you his name but we have a client this guy gives the uh the canadian government or did until he met me gave the canadian government seven excuse me seven hundred thousand dollars a month in hst and payroll that's a lot plus the corporate income tax and on top of all that he actually had to pay the payroll to his staff and then there's rent in the building electricity internet water gas yeah a couple of vehicles we're talking millions of dollars of expenses now what's 700 000 times 12 months Yeah, it's like what, $8.3 million, $8.4 million? That's a lot of money on just tax. Now he pays zero legally, and I own his debt. Now the CRA can chase me for all those millions. Good luck. Good luck.
